next_step_text: Jun 29, 2026 Received documents for RFP round 2 Due date 17.7. Jun 22, 2026 Second round of RFP should be started by end of June at the latest. In parallel implementation partners will be contacted. Next step: call Thomas Krimse on 25.6. Jun 08, 2026 Answered questions on feedback - people out on PTO to follow up on POC and pricing/contracts. May 21, 2026 21.5. Presentation of agentic AI capabilit

our_open_deliverables.text: Send Zycus's written response to the relevant stakeholder evaluation summary
Provide the implementation-partner matrix to Thomas Kirmse

buyer_dependent_items.text: Bosch to close the POC with all vendors, define next strategy and provide commercial next-steps
Bosch to return RFP round 2 evaluation outcome after Zycus responds

competitive_position.summary: Bosch is running a live 4-5 vendor tender with a July award imminent and will 'close the PoC with all market participants' before deciding, so the strongest current threat is the undisclosed finalist field in the active down-select, not any status-quo option. No rival is named on any call or in Salesforce, which remains a genuine knowledge gap and the priority to close with Thomas Kirmse. The clearest characterised alternative is the status quo of fragmented best-of-breed tools the platform would replace. Zycus's differentiators are organically-built agentic AI (Merlin) and proven live SAP S/4HANA integration, both praised in the POC.

competitors.name: Undisclosed finalist(s) in Bosch's 4-5 vendor tender
competitors.quote: We are in a long-list of 4-5 suppliers.
competitors.source: Salesforce Next Step (2 Feb 2026); Thomas Kirmse, Avoma POC closing 22 May 2026 ('close the PoC with all market participants')
competitors.how_we_win: Extract the named shortlist from Kirmse, then shape the RFP round 2 response to the scorecard and position organic agentic AI (Merlin) plus proven live S/4HANA integration as the down-select differentiators.

competitors.name: Status quo / fragmented best-of-breed tools
competitors.quote: not all functionalities meet expectations 100% out of the box... huge potential for customization... seamless sourcing-to-contracting not fully seamless, could lead to cumbersome user experience
competitors.source: Avoma POC closing 22 May 2026 (Rebecca Elias findings)
competitors.how_we_win: Position the unified 12-product S2P suite as single-platform simplification versus tool sprawl, and de-risk the customization load Bosch flagged with a clear implementation and partner plan so status-quo comfort does not win by default.

champion_strength.source: CRM MEDDPICC (13 May 2026); Avoma POC closing 22 May 2026; Salesforce Next Step (22 Jun 2026)
champion_strength.summary: Engagement is broad and operationally strong (Nordmann coordinated the POC, Elias delivered positive findings, Kirmse owns commercials), but there is no confirmed champion actively selling Zycus to the economic buyer. Thomas Kirmse is the designated candidate and route to power yet is thinly present on calls, so champion strength is developing: deep operational advocacy exists, power-level advocacy does not.

sources: Avoma call manifest 19 Feb-26 May 2026 (EB absent)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: After seven months, a $1.2M deal and a two-week on-site POC, the budget owner has never appeared on any recorded call, which is the single biggest structural risk on this deal. Every engaged stakeholder is operational (procurement and IT-process), and on 12 May the note 'John suggested to engage with c level once we are through the current stage of testing' shows C-level contact was consciously deferred; the POC finished 22 May with no C-level engagement since. What the evidence does show is that Thomas Kirmse (Category Purchasing Software & IT Services) owns the commercial and partner thread and is the route to power. Securing an C-level meeting via Kirmse before the July award is a ranked move.

recommended_moves: Send Zycus's written point-by-point response to the relevant stakeholder findings with the SCIM and BYOK commitment.
Submit the RFP round 2 response mapping each Bosch scorecard gap to Merlin and roadmap commitments.
Have the Zycus VP open a meeting with Bosch's budget owner via Thomas Kirmse before the award.
